title: Question: Circular logic declared, on logic that isn't circular
body:
---


Author Name: **Dan Gisselquist**
Original Redmine Message: 2386 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hello,

I'm building an LFSR shift register module that produces multiple-output bits per clock.  (I'm hoping to blog about this shortly at zipcpu.com ...)  To do this, I need to pre-calculate a series of constants--equations that can be evaluated prior to run-time, and then used at runtime.

At issue is a series of wires that are constants--captured by the included file, both those named tapv and the reset_value.  The are calculated from other constants, which are calculated from other constants, which ... trace back to a module parameter.  If declared these values to be as wires, Verilator insists that there is circular logic involved.   If they are instead declared as "registers" by defining WORKS_WITH_VERILATOR, Verilator succeeds just fine while other tools yap about the fact that they never change.

I've attached the code in question.

While my default Verilator version is 3.8(something), I have also tested with the version in the git repository this morning and found the same problem.

I'm not sure if I'd call this a feature request or a bug report ... but it would be nice to see it fixed.

Dan
